title: International Conference on Disaster Resilient Infrastructure (ICDRI) & CDRI
focus: CDRI's structure, ICDRI 2025 outcomes, and India's coastal vulnerability mitigation initiatives.
rationale: CDRI is a major India-led global initiative. Its structure, funds (IRAF), and related coastal initiatives (ICZMP, CVI) are highly relevant for UPSC Prelims.
sectionExcerpt: African Union joined India's CDRI as the 54th member at the 7th ICDRI in France. CDRI was launched by India at the 2019 UN Climate Action Summit to promote infrastructure resilience. It features initiatives like IRIS and the Infrastructure Resilience Accelerator Fund. Indian coastal initiatives include CRZ, ICZMP, and INCOIS's Coastal Vulnerability Index.
evidenceBullets: African Union joined CDRI as the 54th member at the 7th ICDRI in France.
CDRI was launched by India at the 2019 United Nations Climate Action Summit.
India is the permanent co-chair of the CDRI Governing Council.
CDRI initiatives include Infrastructure for Resilient Island States (IRIS) and Infrastructure Resilience Accelerator Fund (IRAF).
Indian National Centre for Ocean Information Services (INCOIS) developed the Coastal Vulnerability Index (CVI) and Multi-Hazard Vulnerability Maps.

title: Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) Financing and UNDRR
focus: India's DRR financing under the 15th Finance Commission, Sendai Framework, and UNDRR's role.
rationale: The 15th Finance Commission's methodology for disaster funds (NDMF/SDMF) and international frameworks like Sendai are core disaster management topics.
sectionExcerpt: UNDRR released the Global Assessment Report titled Resilience Pays. India's DRR financing under the 15th Finance Commission exceeds INR 2.32 trillion, using a new methodology based on a Disaster Risk Index. It recommended the creation of NDMF and SDMF, with the Centre contributing 75% for general states and 90% for NE/Himalayan states. The Sendai Framework (2015-30) succeeded the Hyogo Framework.
evidenceBullets: UNDRR released the Global Assessment Report (GAR) titled Resilience Pays.
15th Finance Commission recommended the creation of National and State Disaster Mitigation Funds (NDMF and SDMF).
Centre contributes 75% to SDMF for general states and 90% for North-Eastern/Himalayan states.
Sendai Framework for DRR (2015-30) is the successor to the Hyogo Framework for Action.
UNDRR, headquartered in Geneva, is the lead UN agency for coordinating DRR and achieving Sendai Framework goals.

title: C-FLOOD Platform for Flood Forecasting
focus: Features, technology, and developing agencies of the C-FLOOD web-based platform.
rationale: Technological interventions in disaster management, specifically flood forecasting tools and their developing agencies, are frequent targets for objective questions.
sectionExcerpt: C-FLOOD is a web-based platform providing 2-day advance flood inundation forecasts up to the village level using advanced 2-D hydrodynamic modelling. It was jointly developed by C-DAC, Central Water Commission (CWC), Ministry of Jal Shakti, and National Remote Sensing Centre (NRSC).
evidenceBullets: C-FLOOD provides 2-day advance flood inundation forecasts up to the village level.
The platform utilizes advanced 2-D hydrodynamic modelling for flood inundation maps and water level predictions.
It was jointly developed by C-DAC, Central Water Commission (CWC), Ministry of Jal Shakti, and NRSC.
